initial_draft:
<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
<my resources> X: 5, Y: 25 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> I have 5 X and 25 Y. The other player has 25 X and 5 Y. They are proposing to give me 10 X in exchange for 1 Y. If I accept, I will end up with 15 X and 24 Y. If I reject, I keep my current resources. Since I want to maximize my total resources and I have a surplus of Y while they have a surplus of X, this trade seems very favorable for me. I will gain 10 X for the loss of only 1 Y. This is a good deal. I will accept.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<message> I accept your trade. It looks like a very good deal for me. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. The response contains all required tags in the correct order with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. The trade (Gain 10 X, Lose 1 Y) results in a net gain of 9 total resources (15+24 vs 5+25), which directly maximizes the goal.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent offered this trade first, so they are highly motivated to accept it to close the game.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason supports the decision to accept, the answer is ACCEPT, the message is consistent, and the proposed trade is NONE.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the first interaction, so the proposal budget is not yet exhausted.
</critique>
